documentation.value:
Initialize a new build stage and set the base image.

```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:22.04
FROM node:20-alpine
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 golang:1.21
```

Set a base image with a named build stage for multi-stage builds.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
RUN npm ci && npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine AS production
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
```

Use an empty base image. Commonly used for statically compiled binaries (Go, Rust).

```dockerfile
FROM scratch
COPY --from=builder /app/mybin /mybin
ENTRYPOINT ["/mybin"]
```

Specify the target platform for the build stage.

```dockerfile
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:22.04
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.21 AS builder
```

completions.label: RUN
completions.detail: Execute a command in the build

documentation.value:
Execute a command in a new layer on top of the current image.

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
```

completions.label: RUN (exec form)
completions.detail: Execute a command in exec form

documentation.value:
Execute a command using the exec form (JSON array). This avoids shell processing and is preferred when you don't need shell features.

```dockerfile
RUN ["executable", "param1", "param2"]
RUN ["apt-get", "install", "-y", "curl"]
```

Mount a cache directory that persists across builds. Speeds up package manager installs.

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip \
    pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Mount a secret file that won't be stored in the final image.

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret,target=/run/secrets/mysecret \
    cat /run/secrets/mysecret

# Build: docker build --secret id=mysecret,src=secret.txt .
```

Mount the SSH agent socket for accessing private repositories.

```dockerfile
RUN --mount=type=ssh \
    git clone git@github.com:private/repo.git

# Build: docker build --ssh default .
```

Set the default command to run when the container starts. Only the last CMD takes effect.

```dockerfile
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

Add metadata as key-value pairs to an image.

```dockerfile
LABEL maintainer="user@example.com"
LABEL version="1.0" description="My app"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/user/repo"
```

**Deprecated.** Use `LABEL maintainer="..."` instead.

```dockerfile
# Deprecated:
MAINTAINER user@example.com

# Preferred:
LABEL maintainer="user@example.com"
```

Inform Docker the container listens on the specified port(s) at runtime. This is documentation only — you still need `-p` to publish.

```dockerfile
EXPOSE 80
EXPOSE 443
EXPOSE 3000/tcp 3001/udp
```

Set an environment variable that persists in the running container.

```dockerfile
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV APP_HOME=/app
ENV PATH="/app/bin:$PATH"
```

Copy files, directories, or remote URLs into the image. Supports auto-extraction of tar archives. Prefer COPY unless you need tar extraction or URLs.

```dockerfile
ADD app.tar.gz /app/
ADD https://example.com/file.txt /data/
ADD --chown=1000:1000 files/ /app/
```

Copy files or directories from the build context into the image.

```dockerfile
COPY . /app/
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
COPY --chown=node:node src/ /app/src/
```

completions.label: COPY --from
completions.detail: Copy files from another build stage

documentation.value:
Copy files from a named build stage or external image.

```dockerfile
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY --from=0 /app/bin /usr/local/bin/
COPY --from=alpine:latest /etc/alpine-release /
```

Copy files and set ownership in a single step.

```dockerfile
COPY --chown=node:node . /app/
COPY --chown=1000:1000 config/ /etc/app/
```

Set the main executable for the container. CMD arguments are appended to ENTRYPOINT.

```dockerfile
ENTRYPOINT ["docker-entrypoint.sh"]
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "flask"]
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
```

Create a mount point for externally mounted volumes or other containers.

```dockerfile
VOLUME /data
VOLUME ["/var/log", "/data"]
```

completions.label: USER
completions.detail: Set the user for subsequent instructions

documentation.value:
Set the user (and optionally group) for RUN, CMD, and ENTRYPOINT instructions that follow.

```dockerfile
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser appuser
USER appuser
USER 1000:1000
```

Set the working directory for RUN, CMD, ENTRYPOINT, COPY, and ADD instructions that follow.

```dockerfile
WORKDIR /app
WORKDIR /usr/src/app
```

Define a variable that users can pass at build time with `--build-arg`.

```dockerfile
ARG NODE_VERSION=20
ARG APP_VERSION
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION}-alpine
```

Build: `docker build --build-arg NODE_VERSION=18 .`

completions.label: ONBUILD
completions.detail: Add trigger instruction for downstream builds

documentation.value:
Add a trigger instruction that executes when the image is used as a base for another build.

```dockerfile
ONBUILD COPY . /app/src
ONBUILD RUN npm install
```

completions.label: STOPSIGNAL
completions.detail: Set the system call signal for stopping the container

documentation.value:
Set the signal that will be sent to the container to stop it.

```dockerfile
STOPSIGNAL SIGTERM
STOPSIGNAL SIGQUIT
STOPSIGNAL 9
```

Tell Docker how to test the container to check that it is still working.

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

HEALTHCHECK NONE
```

Override the default shell used for the shell form of commands.

```dockerfile
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
SHELL ["powershell", "-command"]
```

Use the official Node.js image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine
FROM node:18-slim
FROM node:20-bookworm
```

Variants: `alpine` (minimal ~50MB), `slim` (Debian minimal), `bookworm`/`bullseye` (full Debian).

Use the official Python image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim
FROM python:3.11-alpine
FROM python:3.12-bookworm
```

Variants: `slim` (recommended for most apps), `alpine` (smallest), `bookworm` (full Debian).

Use the official Go image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.22-alpine
FROM golang:1.22-bookworm
```

Go binaries are statically linked by default — ideal for multi-stage builds with `FROM scratch`.

Use the official Rust image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM rust:1.77-slim
FROM rust:1.77-alpine
FROM rust:1.77-bookworm
```

Use multi-stage builds to create minimal final images.

Use the official Ubuntu image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:22.04
FROM ubuntu:24.04
FROM ubuntu:20.04
```

Use Alpine Linux (~5MB) for minimal images.

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:3.19
FROM alpine:3.18
```

Uses `musl` libc and `apk` package manager. May have compatibility issues with some C libraries.

Use the official Debian image as the base.

```dockerfile
FROM debian:bookworm-slim
FROM debian:bullseye-slim
```

`-slim` variants are much smaller and sufficient for most applications.

Use the official Nginx image.

```dockerfile
FROM nginx:alpine
FROM nginx:1.25-alpine
```

Default serves `/usr/share/nginx/html`. Config at `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf`.

Use the official Redis image.

```dockerfile
FROM redis:7-alpine
FROM redis:7-bookworm
```

Data directory: `/data`. Default port: 6379.

Use the official PostgreSQL image.

```dockerfile
FROM postgres:16-alpine
FROM postgres:15-bookworm
```

Set `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `POSTGRES_USER`, `POSTGRES_DB` environment variables. Init scripts in `/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/`.

Use the official MySQL image.

```dockerfile
FROM mysql:8.0
FROM mysql:8.3
```

Set `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD`, `MYSQL_DATABASE`, `MYSQL_USER`, `MYSQL_PASSWORD`. Init scripts in `/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/`.

completions.label: apt-get install pattern
completions.detail: Best practice apt-get install with cleanup

documentation.value:
Install packages with apt-get following Docker best practices: combine update+install, use `--no-install-recommends`, and clean up in a single RUN layer.

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

completions.insertText:
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ${1:package} \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

completions.label: pip install pattern

documentation.value:
Install Python packages with pip following best practices: use `--no-cache-dir` to keep image small.

```dockerfile
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
```

completions.insertText:
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

completions.label: npm install pattern
completions.detail: Best practice npm install with layer caching

documentation.value:
Copy package files first to leverage Docker layer caching. Dependencies are only reinstalled when package files change.

```dockerfile
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
COPY . .
```

completions.label: COPY package.json first
completions.detail: Layer caching pattern for package managers

documentation.value:
Copy dependency manifests before the rest of the code to leverage Docker's build cache. This way, dependencies are only reinstalled when the manifest changes.

```dockerfile
# Dependencies are cached unless package.json changes
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Application code changes don't invalidate deps cache
COPY . .
```

completions.insertText:
# Install dependencies (cached unless manifest changes)
COPY ${1:package.json} ${2:package-lock.json} ./
RUN ${3:npm ci}

# Copy application source
COPY . .

completions.label: ARG + ENV pattern
completions.detail: Use ARG to set ENV at build time

documentation.value:
Combine ARG and ENV to set environment variables that can be overridden at build time and persist at runtime.

```dockerfile
ARG APP_VERSION=1.0.0
ENV APP_VERSION=${APP_VERSION}
```

`ARG` is only available during build. `ENV` persists in the running container.

Configure a health check with interval, timeout, start period, and retries.

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=40s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
```

Options:
- `--interval=DURATION` (default 30s)
- `--timeout=DURATION` (default 30s)
- `--start-period=DURATION` (default 0s)
- `--retries=N` (default 3)

completions.label: Multi-stage builder pattern

documentation.value:
Use a multi-stage build to compile/build in one stage and copy artifacts to a minimal final image.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm ci && npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

completions.label: Non-root user pattern
completions.detail: Create and switch to non-root user

documentation.value:
Security best practice: run the final container as a non-root user.

```dockerfile
RUN addgroup --system appgroup && adduser --system --ingroup appgroup appuser
USER appuser
```

completions.label: Dockerfile: Node.js app
completions.detail: Complete Dockerfile for a Node.js application

documentation.value:
Production-ready Dockerfile for a Node.js application with multi-stage build, non-root user, and health check.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine
RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/package.json ./
USER app
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --retries=3 CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM node:${1:20-alpine} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:${1:20-alpine}
RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/package.json ./
USER app
EXPOSE ${2:3000}
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:${2:3000}/health || exit 1
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]

completions.label: Dockerfile: Python app
completions.detail: Complete Dockerfile for a Python application

documentation.value:
Production-ready Dockerfile for a Python application with virtual env, non-root user, and best practices.

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$PATH"
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.12-slim
RUN useradd --create-home appuser
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$PATH"
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .
USER appuser
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python", "-m", "uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM python:${1:3.12-slim} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$$PATH"
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

FROM python:${1:3.12-slim}
RUN useradd --create-home appuser
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$$PATH"
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .
USER appuser
EXPOSE ${2:8000}
CMD ["python", "-m", "${3:uvicorn}", "${4:main:app}", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "${2:8000}"]

completions.label: Dockerfile: Go app
completions.detail: Complete Dockerfile for a Go application

documentation.value:
Production-ready multi-stage Dockerfile for a Go application using scratch as the final image.

```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags='-w -s' -o /app/server ./cmd/server

FROM scratch
COPY --from=builder /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt /etc/ssl/certs/
COPY --from=builder /app/server /server
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["/server"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM golang:${1:1.22-alpine} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags='-w -s' -o /app/${2:server} ./${3:cmd/server}

FROM scratch
COPY --from=builder /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt /etc/ssl/certs/
COPY --from=builder /app/${2:server} /${2:server}
EXPOSE ${4:8080}
ENTRYPOINT ["/${2:server}"]

completions.label: Dockerfile: Multi-stage build

documentation.value:
A generic multi-stage build template. First stage builds, second stage runs.

```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM image:tag AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN make build

# Production stage
FROM image:tag
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/output /app/
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["./app"]
```

completions.insertText:
# Build stage
FROM ${1:image}:${2:tag} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN ${3:make build}

# Production stage
FROM ${4:image}:${5:tag}
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/${6:output} /app/
EXPOSE ${7:8080}
CMD ["${8:./app}"]

completions.label: Dockerfile: Rust app
completions.detail: Complete Dockerfile for a Rust application

documentation.value:
Production-ready multi-stage Dockerfile for a Rust application.

```dockerfile
FROM rust:1.77-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY Cargo.toml Cargo.lock ./
RUN mkdir src && echo 'fn main(){}' > src/main.rs && cargo build --release && rm -rf src
COPY src ./src
RUN cargo build --release

FROM debian:bookworm-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY --from=builder /app/target/release/myapp /usr/local/bin/myapp
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["myapp"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM rust:${1:1.77-slim} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY Cargo.toml Cargo.lock ./
RUN mkdir src && echo 'fn main(){}' > src/main.rs && cargo build --release && rm -rf src
COPY src ./src
RUN cargo build --release

FROM debian:bookworm-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY --from=builder /app/target/release/${2:myapp} /usr/local/bin/${2:myapp}
EXPOSE ${3:8080}
CMD ["${2:myapp}"]

completions.label: Dockerfile: Nginx static site
completions.detail: Dockerfile for serving static files with Nginx

documentation.value:
Serve a static website or SPA with Nginx.

```dockerfile
FROM nginx:alpine
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY dist/ /usr/share/nginx/html/
EXPOSE 80
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s CMD wget -qO- http://localhost/ || exit 1
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM nginx:alpine
COPY ${1:nginx.conf} /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY ${2:dist/} /usr/share/nginx/html/
EXPOSE 80
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost/ || exit 1
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

documentation.value:
Install packages on Alpine Linux with `--no-cache` to avoid caching the index.

```dockerfile
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    tzdata
```

completions.label: ENTRYPOINT + CMD pattern
completions.detail: ENTRYPOINT with CMD as default args

documentation.value:
Use ENTRYPOINT for the executable and CMD for default arguments. Users can override CMD args at `docker run`.

```dockerfile
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "app.py"]
CMD ["--port", "8000"]

# docker run myimage                  -> python app.py --port 8000
# docker run myimage --port 9000      -> python app.py --port 9000
```

completions.label: .dockerignore note

documentation.value:
Always create a `.dockerignore` file to exclude files from the build context, reducing build time and image size.

```
# .dockerignore
node_modules
.git
.env
*.md
Dockerfile
.dockerignore
dist
coverage
.nyc_output
```

completions.insertText:
# Remember to create a .dockerignore file:
# node_modules
# .git
# .env
# *.md
# Dockerfile
# .dockerignore

documentation.value:
Use `--link` to create an independent layer that doesn't depend on previous layers, enabling better cache reuse.

```dockerfile
COPY --link package.json ./
COPY --link --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
```

Available since BuildKit. The layer is not invalidated by changes to prior layers.

Disable network access during a RUN step for security.

```dockerfile
RUN --network=none cat /etc/hostname
```

Apply standardized OCI annotations to your image.

```dockerfile
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="My App" \
      org.opencontainers.image.description="Description" \
      org.opencontainers.image.version="1.0.0" \
      org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/user/repo" \
      org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"
```

completions.label: Dockerfile: Java Spring Boot app
completions.detail: Dockerfile for Java Spring Boot using layered JARs

documentation.value:
Multi-stage Dockerfile for a Spring Boot application with dependency layer caching.

```dockerfile
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN ./mvnw package -DskipTests

FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/target/*.jar app.jar
RUN addgroup -S spring && adduser -S spring -G spring
USER spring
EXPOSE 8080
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --retries=3 CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
```

completions.insertText:
FROM eclipse-temurin:${1:21-jdk-alpine} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN ./mvnw package -DskipTests

FROM eclipse-temurin:${2:21-jre-alpine}
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/target/*.jar app.jar
RUN addgroup -S spring && adduser -S spring -G spring
USER spring
EXPOSE ${3:8080}
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:${3:8080}/actuator/health || exit 1
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
